name: Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón
region_intro: Jogging routes Malagón offer a diverse landscape for runners, ranging from flat, open areas to more undulating terrain. The region features a mix of natural parks, such as the wetlands of Las Tablas de Daimiel, and the more elevated areas of the Sierra de Malagón. These varied environments provide options for different running preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Malagón The most popular jogging route is Las Tablas de Damiel — circular por el Parque Nacional, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a flat, circular path through a national park. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This route provides a challenging experience with significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Malagón. Local runners also love the Malagón loop from Malagón, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail leading through the local surroundings, often completed in about 47 minutes. Jogging around Malagón is defined by wetlands, hills, and recreational areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Malagón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Malagón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Malagón?
A: Yes, Malagón offers several moderate running routes that are suitable for beginners looking for a good workout without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210329865/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-por-el-parque-nacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel — circular por el Parque Nacional&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively flat, circular path through a national park, ideal for an accessible run.

Q: Can I find shorter running routes, for example, around 5k, in the Malagón area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Malagón are well-suited for shorter distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210329865/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-por-el-parque-nacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel — circular por el Parque Nacional&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 7.9 km (4.9 miles), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malagón loop from Malagón&lt;/a&gt; is about 7.6 km (4.7 miles), both offering excellent options for a run around or slightly above 5k.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Malagón?
A: Yes, many of the running trails around Malagón are designed as circular routes, which are great for starting and ending in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210329865/las-tablas-de-damiel-circular-por-el-parque-nacional" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tablas de Damiel — circular por el Parque Nacional&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211535332/sierra-de-malagon-circular-por-las-navas-de-malagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Malagón?
A: The Malagón region offers a diverse landscape for runners. You can expect a mix of flat, open areas, especially around the wetlands of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt;, and more undulating terrain with significant elevation gain in the elevated areas of the Sierra de Malagón. This variety caters to different running preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any scenic running routes with viewpoints in Malagón?
A: Yes, the region boasts scenic routes, particularly those venturing into the Sierra de Malagón. While specific viewpoints on running routes aren't always marked, the natural beauty of the area, including highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Observation point&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3582372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; near Tablas de Daimiel, offers picturesque backdrops for your run.

Q: What are some natural attractions or landmarks I might see while running in Malagón?
A: Runners in Malagón can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a significant wetland area, and you might also come across natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Hervideros&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking higher ground, the region also features mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Picón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Malagón?
A: The running routes in Malagón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from flat national park paths to challenging mountain trails, and the opportunity to explore both wetlands and elevated areas.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through recreational areas?
A: Yes, some routes offer access to recreational areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chaparrillo Recreation Area loop from Malagón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4 km (4.6 miles) route that takes you through local surroundings, including this recreational space.

Q: How many running routes are there in total around Malagón?
A: There are approximately 8 running routes available around Malagón, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Malagón?
A: Malagón's climate generally allows for running year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in open areas, while winters are mild, making them suitable for running, particularly on routes with less exposure.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners in Malagón?
A: Yes, experienced runners looking for a challenge will find options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211535332/sierra-de-malagon-circular-por-las-navas-de-malagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Malagón — circular por Las Navas de Malagón&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 18.2 km (11.3 miles) route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra de Malagón, providing a demanding experience.
tips: The Plaza de los Moros is one of the two important peaks of the Sierra de Malagón trail. The other peak is Cerro Amarillo and it is a short distance down the same trail from here. It's all uphill up here, but the views are the reward.
